content.full_text:
August 16, 1976

A-76-480

The Honorable Evelle J. Younger
Attorney General of the State
of California
555 Capitol Mall, Suite 550
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Attorney General Younger:

     This is to acknowledge receipt of your
letter of July 26 approving the Commission's
Conflict of Interest Code subject to three
minor revisions previously discussed and
agreed to by the staffs of our respective
agencies.  The revisions called for have been
incorporated into our Code.

     Thank you for your assistance in this
matter.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                        Chairman

DHL:cjb
bcc: Jeanne ∟

Aurora

[illegible]

Council of the Confederated
Tribes and Bands of the Yakima Indian Nation
of Confederated
[illegible] Tribes of the Colville Indian
Reservation, Colville, [illegible]

Dear Aurotew Carson:

I am in receipt of your letter of June 19, 1975.

The Intergovernmental Tax Compact (Interstate Compact)
passed without objection to the Committee for Economic
Development under House Bill 326. The Committee on
Legislative Affairs of the Western Regional Legislative
Leaders Conference also approved the measure for two
years. The Legislative Council may pass it for the
next Legislative session.

I want you to assure tribal groups in this area.

Medicine

Daniel H. Biedrzycki
Chairman
Orchid

Fort Simcoe - August 1 Dispatch
[illegible]

EVELLE J. YOUNGER
ATTORNEY GENERAL

STATE OF CALIFORNIA

OFFICE OF THE ATTORNEY GENERAL

Department of Justice
555 CAPITOL MALL, SUITE 550
SACRAMENTO 95814
(916) 445-9555

July 26, 1976


Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street, Second Floor
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: FPPC's Conflict of Interest Code

Dear Chairman Lowenstein:

Pursuant to Government Code section 87303, I hereby
approve the Conflict of Interest Code submitted by the
Fair Political Practices Commission to me on May 3, 1976,
with certain minor revisions which have been mutually
discussed between our two agencies.

Such revisions are as follows:

1. On page three, under "Category C," the position
entitled "Political Reform Analyst" is deleted as
a designated employee position.

2. On page three, in section 410(a), the reference
to the "California Department of Justice" is revised
to "California Attorney General."

3. On pages five and six, under "Group B" and
"Group C," the phrase ". . . in the future fore-
seeably might . . ." is revised to ". . . in the
future with reasonable foreseeability might . . ."

Very truly yours,

EVELLE J. YOUNGER
Attorney General

ac
